question.content: As shown in the figure, in triangle ABC, AB > AC. Two points X and Y inside triangle ABC both lie on the angle bisector of angle BAC, and satisfy angle ABX = angle ACY. Let the extension of BX intersect segment CY at point P. The circumcircle omega_1 of triangle BPY and the circumcircle omega_2 of triangle CPX intersect at P and another point Q. Find the measure of angle APQ. Answer format requirement: <expr>. Strictly forbidden: any extra text beyond this format.

step_1.content: In this construction, Q is taken on the extension of AP, so A, P, and Q are collinear.
step_1.key_point: Once A, P, and Q lie on one line, the target angle is a straight-angle check at P.

step_2.content: At vertex P, angle APQ is formed by ray PA and ray PQ.
step_2.key_point: Because A, P, and Q are collinear, ray PA and ray PQ are opposite rays on line AQ.

step_4.content: An angle formed by a pair of opposite rays is a straight angle.
